title: Restrict rotation CronJob ServiceAccount to read-only
description:
# GitHub Issue #190: Restrict rotation CronJob ServiceAccount to read-only and let operator handle Secret writes

**Category**: security | **Scope**: Medium

**Description**: Refactor the fernet/credential key rotation architecture so that the CronJob ServiceAccount only has read access to the key Secrets. The CronJob should compute the new key set and output it (e.g., to a temporary ConfigMap or annotation), and the operator's reconcile loop should detect the output and perform the actual Secret update using its own privileged ServiceAccount.

**Motivation**: The current rotation CronJob's Role grants get and patch on fernet-keys or credential-keys Secrets. If the CronJob pod is compromised via a supply chain attack on the Keystone image, the attacker can write arbitrary fernet keys enabling token forgery. Separating compute (CronJob) from write (operator) follows least-privilege principles.

**Affected Areas**:
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_fernet.go`
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_credential.go`

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] CronJob ServiceAccount has only read access to key Secrets
- [ ] Operator reconciler detects rotation output and performs the Secret update
- [ ] Rotation workflow functions identically from an end-user perspective
- [ ] Unit test verifies CronJob RBAC does not include patch/update verbs on Secrets
- [ ] E2E test verifies key rotation completes successfully with the new architecture

---
Source: https://github.com/C5C3/forge/issues/190

stories.title: Security engineer restricts CronJob SA to read-only on key Secrets
stories.want: the Fernet and credential rotation CronJob ServiceAccounts to have NO write verbs on the key Secrets
stories.so_that: a supply-chain compromise of the Keystone image cannot forge tokens by writing arbitrary Fernet or credential keys to the production Secret
criteria: Role `{name}-fernet-rotate` exposes only the `get` verb on the fernet-keys Secret; no `patch`, `update`, `create`, or `delete` verbs appear (CC-0081)
Role `{name}-credential-rotate` exposes only the `get` verb on the credential-keys Secret; no `patch`, `update`, `create`, or `delete` verbs appear (CC-0081)
`resourceNames` in each Role is scoped to the specific keys Secret and the specific staging Secret, not wildcarded
A unit test `TestEnsureFernetRotationRBAC_MainSecretReadOnly` (and credential counterpart) asserts that no PolicyRule grants write verbs on `{name}-fernet-keys` or `{name}-credential-keys` (CC-0081)

stories.title: Operator applies rotated keys from the staging Secret to the production Secret
stories.want: to detect a completed rotation output produced by the CronJob and apply it to the production keys Secret using the operator ServiceAccount
stories.so_that: the write path to the production Secret is owned by the operator, not by the rotation job
criteria: When a staging Secret `{name}-fernet-keys-rotation` (or `{name}-credential-keys-rotation`) exists with annotation `forge.c5c3.io/rotation-completed-at`, `reconcileFernetKeys` / `reconcileCredentialKeys` copies its `data` into the production Secret via `Patch` (CC-0081)
After a successful apply, the operator deletes the staging Secret to avoid re-applying stale output
If the staging Secret has no completion annotation, the operator does NOT touch the production Secret (partial writes must not leak into production)
The operator records a Kubernetes Event `FernetKeysRotated` / `CredentialKeysRotated` with the observed key count on successful apply (CC-0081)

The `FernetKeysReady` / `CredentialKeysReady` condition transitions from False with reason `RotationPending` while a staging Secret is present but fails validation, and returns to True with reason `FernetKeysAvailable` after a successful apply

stories.title: Operator rejects malformed or tampered rotation output
stories.want: to validate the staging Secret content before applying it to the production Secret
stories.so_that: a compromised CronJob that writes malformed or out-of-policy keys into the staging Secret still cannot corrupt the production Secret
criteria: Validation fails if any staged key is not a 44-character base64url string with padding (same format as `generateFernetKey`) (CC-0081)

Validation fails if the staging Secret contains fewer than 3 keys or more than `normalizedFernetMaxActiveKeys(keystone) + 1` keys (the +1 tolerates the transient rotation state observed between `fernet_rotate` and key pruning)
Validation fails if any two staged keys are byte-identical

On validation failure: the operator does not patch the production Secret, emits event `RotationRejected` with the reason, sets `FernetKeysReady` / `CredentialKeysReady` to False with reason `RotationInvalid`, and leaves the staging Secret in place so a human can inspect it
A unit test covers each validation failure mode independently (CC-0081)

stories.title: CronJob writes rotated keys to a dedicated staging Secret
stories.want: to write the rotated key set to a dedicated per-Keystone staging Secret over the Kubernetes API using its own narrowly-scoped ServiceAccount
stories.so_that: compute (the CronJob) is separated from write-to-production (the operator), preserving least-privilege
criteria: `fernet_rotate.sh` PATCHes `{name}-fernet-keys-rotation` (the staging Secret) instead of `{name}-fernet-keys` (CC-0081)
`credential_rotate.sh` PATCHes `{name}-credential-keys-rotation` instead of `{name}-credential-keys` (CC-0081)
Both scripts add annotation `forge.c5c3.io/rotation-completed-at=<RFC3339 UTC timestamp>` on the PATCH to signal a complete write
The `SECRET_NAME` environment variable passed to the container matches the staging Secret name, not the production Secret name
A unit test asserts the CronJob pod spec's `SECRET_NAME` env var equals the staging Secret name for both Fernet and credential CronJobs (CC-0081)

stories.title: Operator owns the staging Secret lifecycle
stories.want: to create the staging Secret eagerly (empty) with an owner reference, and to delete it automatically after a successful apply or on Keystone deletion
stories.so_that: the CronJob can only PATCH an existing, operator-owned Secret (never `create`), and stale staging Secrets do not accumulate
criteria: Reconcile creates `{name}-fernet-keys-rotation` and `{name}-credential-keys-rotation` with `controllerutil.SetControllerReference(keystone, ...)` so both are GC'd when the Keystone CR is deleted (CC-0081)
The staging Secret is created with empty `data` and label `forge.c5c3.io/rotation-target=<fernet-keys|credential-keys>`
Staging Secrets persist across reconciles; the operator deletes them only after successfully copying their `data` into the production Secret
CronJob Role grants `get` and `patch` on the staging Secret (scoped by `resourceNames`), but NOT `create` or `delete` (CC-0081)

stories.title: End-user observes an unchanged rotation workflow
stories.want: the externally observable behavior of key rotation to remain the same after this refactor
stories.so_that: upgrading the operator does not require changes to my runbooks or alerting
criteria: The rotation CronJob schedule continues to match `spec.fernet.rotationSchedule` and `spec.credentialKeys.rotationSchedule`
A manual rotation via `kubectl create job --from=cronjob/{name}-fernet-rotate` still ends with the production `{name}-fernet-keys` Secret containing a new key set
Existing pre-rotation Fernet tokens remain valid after rotation (multi-key overlap preserved, see CC-0074) (CC-0081)
Credential rotation continues to run `credential_rotate` + `credential_migrate` in that order so existing DB-encrypted credentials remain decryptable
The existing `PushSecret` backup to OpenBao continues to operate against the production Secret

stories.title: Developer writes a Reference doc for the split-compute-write architecture
stories.want: the reconciler reference documentation to describe the staging Secret, the completion annotation, validation rules, and the new RBAC boundaries
stories.so_that: future maintainers understand why the CronJob does not have write access on the production Secret
criteria: `docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md` is updated with a new subsection `Key Rotation RBAC Split (CC-0081)` under both the Fernet and credential reconciler sections
Diagram or table documents: input (read-only volume), compute (keystone-manage), staging write path, operator apply path, output (production Secret, PushSecret)
Validation rules for rotation output are listed (format, count, distinctness)
RBAC table lists the Role's verbs+resourceNames for both main and staging Secrets

stories.title: Developer writes a How-to guide for rotating manually under the new architecture
stories.want: a how-to guide for triggering manual rotation and for recovering from a rejected rotation
stories.so_that: I can safely trigger and audit rotation without reading the Go source
criteria: `docs/how-to/` (or existing keystone how-to file) gains steps for: trigger manual rotation, verify staging Secret completion annotation, verify operator apply, verify main Secret data changed
Guide documents the `RotationRejected` event and points to the validation rules
Guide documents staging Secret cleanup (automatic on apply; manual intervention steps if the operator is disabled)

requirements.description: The Fernet rotation Role SHALL grant only the `get` verb on the production Fernet keys Secret (no `patch`, `update`, `create`, or `delete`).
requirements.rationale: CC-0081: A CronJob pod compromised via supply-chain attack must not be able to write arbitrary Fernet keys; restricting to `get` removes the token-forgery primitive from the CronJob's attack surface.

scenarios.name: Role rules for main Secret are read-only
scenarios.when: `ensureFernetRotationRBAC` is called with `secretName="ks-fernet-keys"`
scenarios.then: the resulting Role's PolicyRules contain at most the verb `get` for `resourceNames=["ks-fernet-keys"]`
and_then: no PolicyRule in the Role enumerates `patch`, `update`, `create`, or `delete` against `ks-fernet-keys`
no PolicyRule uses a `*` verb or omits `resourceNames` for the production Secret

scenarios.name: Existing Role is downgraded on upgrade
scenarios.when: a pre-CC-0081 Role with verbs `[get, patch]` on the production Secret exists and reconcile runs against the updated operator
scenarios.then: the Role is updated so the production Secret rule contains only `get`
and_then: the RoleBinding is preserved
the ServiceAccount is preserved

scenarios.name: `list` and `watch` verbs are NOT added
scenarios.when: `ensureFernetRotationRBAC` runs
scenarios.then: the Role does NOT grant `list` or `watch` on Secrets (the CronJob reads via projected volume, not the API)
and_then: the absence prevents the compromised CronJob from enumerating other Secrets in the namespace

requirements.description: The credential rotation Role SHALL grant only the `get` verb on the production credential-keys Secret.
requirements.rationale: CC-0081: Symmetric with REQ-001; credential keys encrypt long-lived application credentials in the DB — unauthorized writes are equally dangerous.

scenarios.name: Role rules for main credential Secret are read-only
scenarios.when: `ensureCredentialRotationRBAC` is called with `secretName="ks-credential-keys"`
scenarios.then: the resulting Role's PolicyRules contain at most the verb `get` for `resourceNames=["ks-credential-keys"]`
and_then: no write verbs appear for `ks-credential-keys`

scenarios.name: Unchanged credential_migrate pod capability
scenarios.when: the credential rotation CronJob runs
scenarios.then: `keystone-manage credential_migrate` still executes successfully because it reads keys from the projected volume, not via the API
and_then: no DB-level permission changes are required

scenarios.name: `delete` and `deletecollection` absent
scenarios.when: `ensureCredentialRotationRBAC` runs
scenarios.then: the Role does not grant `delete` or `deletecollection` on Secrets
and_then: a compromised CronJob cannot remove the production Secret to trigger a re-generation race

requirements.description: The rotation Role SHALL grant `get` and `patch` on a dedicated staging Secret scoped by `resourceNames`, and SHALL NOT grant `create` or `delete` on it.
requirements.rationale: CC-0081: The operator owns the staging Secret lifecycle (create + delete); the CronJob only patches data into it. This keeps the principle of least-privilege by minimizing the CronJob's verb surface.

scenarios.name: Staging Secret rule is narrow
scenarios.when: `ensureFernetRotationRBAC` runs for keystone `ks`
scenarios.then: a PolicyRule grants `[get, patch]` on `resourceNames=["ks-fernet-keys-rotation"]`
and_then: no broader Secret verbs are present
the staging Secret rule and the main Secret rule are separate PolicyRules

scenarios.name: CronJob cannot create arbitrary Secrets
scenarios.when: a compromised CronJob pod attempts to POST `/api/v1/namespaces/ns/secrets`
scenarios.then: the API server denies the request with 403 (the ServiceAccount lacks `create` on secrets)

scenarios.name: CronJob cannot delete the staging Secret
scenarios.when: a compromised CronJob attempts to DELETE the staging Secret
scenarios.then: the API server denies the request
and_then: this forces any adversarial behavior to be visible in the staging Secret for operator inspection

requirements.description: The operator SHALL create an empty, owner-referenced staging Secret for each key-family (fernet, credential) on every reconcile.
requirements.rationale: CC-0081: The staging Secret must exist before the CronJob runs (otherwise PATCH returns 404 and the CronJob lacks `create`). Using an owner reference ensures GC when the Keystone CR is deleted.

scenarios.name: Staging Secret created when absent
scenarios.when: reconcile runs and `{name}-fernet-keys-rotation` does not exist
scenarios.then: the operator creates it with empty `Data`, label `forge.c5c3.io/rotation-target=fernet-keys`, and an owner reference to the Keystone CR
and_then: the next reconcile finds and reuses the same Secret

scenarios.name: Staging Secret left alone when present
scenarios.when: reconcile runs and the staging Secret exists without the completion annotation
scenarios.then: the operator leaves the staging Secret untouched
and_then: no spurious updates, no event emissions

scenarios.name: Staging Secret GC'd on Keystone deletion
scenarios.when: the Keystone CR is deleted
scenarios.then: Kubernetes garbage collection removes the staging Secret via the owner reference
and_then: no manual cleanup is required

requirements.description: The operator SHALL apply rotated keys from the staging Secret to the production Secret ONLY when the staging Secret carries annotation `forge.c5c3.io/rotation-completed-at` with a valid RFC3339 timestamp.
requirements.rationale: CC-0081: The annotation is a single-shot commit marker written by the CronJob script after a successful rotation+validation pass. Without it, the operator must not observe partial writes.

scenarios.name: Completion annotation present — apply
scenarios.when: the staging Secret has `data` = {"0":..., "1":..., "2":...} and annotation `forge.c5c3.io/rotation-completed-at=2026-04-18T10:00:00Z`
scenarios.then: the operator PATCHes the production Secret's `data` to match and deletes the staging Secret
and_then: a `FernetKeysRotated` / `CredentialKeysRotated` event is emitted on the Keystone CR with the key count

scenarios.name: Completion annotation absent — skip
scenarios.when: the staging Secret has `data` but no completion annotation
scenarios.then: the operator does not modify the production Secret and does not delete the staging Secret
and_then: reconcile continues to the CronJob and PushSecret steps without error

scenarios.name: Malformed annotation — skip + warn
scenarios.when: the completion annotation is present but is not a valid RFC3339 timestamp
scenarios.then: the operator emits a `RotationAnnotationInvalid` warning event and treats the staging Secret as pending (no apply, no delete)
and_then: reconcile does not return an error — a human can inspect

requirements.description: The operator SHALL validate rotation output before applying and SHALL reject invalid output without mutating the production Secret.
requirements.rationale: CC-0081: Defense in depth — even with RBAC restriction, the operator-side validation ensures a compromised CronJob cannot push malformed or adversarial keys.

scenarios.name: Key format valid
scenarios.when: all staged keys decode as 32-byte base64url with padding (44 chars) and are distinct
scenarios.then: validation passes
and_then: the apply proceeds

scenarios.name: Wrong-length key rejected
scenarios.when: any staged key has length other than 44 or fails base64url decode
scenarios.then: validation fails with error `invalid key length` and the production Secret is NOT patched
and_then: event `RotationRejected` emitted with the offending key index
staging Secret is left in place

scenarios.name: Duplicate keys rejected
scenarios.when: two staged keys have identical bytes
scenarios.then: validation fails with error `duplicate keys detected`
and_then: production Secret not patched

scenarios.name: Key count out of range rejected
scenarios.when: the staging Secret contains fewer than 3 keys or more than `MaxActiveKeys + 1`
scenarios.then: validation fails with error `key count out of range: got N, want [3, M+1]`
and_then: production Secret not patched

requirements.description: The Fernet rotation script `fernet_rotate.sh` SHALL PATCH the staging Secret (not the production Secret) and SHALL include the `forge.c5c3.io/rotation-completed-at` annotation in the PATCH payload.
requirements.rationale: CC-0081: The script is the write-side artifact; it must target staging only.

scenarios.name: PATCH targets staging Secret
scenarios.when: the script runs in the CronJob pod with `SECRET_NAME=ks-fernet-keys-rotation`
scenarios.then: the HTTP PATCH URL is `/api/v1/namespaces/<ns>/secrets/ks-fernet-keys-rotation`
and_then: no reference to the production Secret name appears in the PATCH payload or URL

scenarios.name: PATCH body includes metadata.annotations
scenarios.when: the script builds the PATCH JSON
scenarios.then: the body contains `metadata.annotations["forge.c5c3.io/rotation-completed-at"]` with an RFC3339 UTC timestamp
and_then: the annotation is ALWAYS written atomically with `data`

scenarios.name: Script fails loudly on 403
scenarios.when: the API server returns 403 (e.g., RBAC misconfiguration)
scenarios.then: the script exits with a non-zero status and prints `Secret update failed: 403 <body>`
and_then: the CronJob transitions to Failed via `backoffLimit`

requirements.description: The credential rotation script `credential_rotate.sh` SHALL behave symmetrically to `fernet_rotate.sh` but SHALL continue to run `credential_rotate` followed by `credential_migrate` BEFORE the staging-Secret PATCH.
requirements.rationale: CC-0081, CC-0036: `credential_migrate` must re-encrypt DB-stored credentials with the new primary key while the emptyDir still contains both old and new keys. Reordering would break decryption of existing credentials.

scenarios.name: migrate before PATCH
scenarios.when: the script runs
scenarios.then: `keystone-manage credential_rotate` executes, then `keystone-manage credential_migrate`, then the HTTP PATCH to staging
and_then: if `credential_migrate` fails, PATCH is NOT issued and the script exits non-zero

scenarios.name: PATCH targets staging credential Secret
scenarios.when: the script runs
scenarios.then: the PATCH URL references `{name}-credential-keys-rotation`, not `{name}-credential-keys`

scenarios.name: Annotation written with staging PATCH
scenarios.when: the PATCH payload is built
scenarios.then: `forge.c5c3.io/rotation-completed-at` is set to the current UTC timestamp

requirements.description: The operator SHALL watch the staging Secret so that a completed rotation triggers reconcile within one controller-runtime event loop iteration.
requirements.rationale: CC-0081: The apply step must be prompt — the window between `credential_migrate` (DB now uses new primary) and the production Secret update defines authentication-failure risk.

scenarios.name: Staging Secret update enqueues Keystone
scenarios.when: the CronJob PATCHes the staging Secret with completion annotation
scenarios.then: the existing `secretToKeystoneMapper` enqueues the owning Keystone CR for reconcile
and_then: the reconcile picks up the staged keys in the next pass

scenarios.name: Orphan staging Secret ignored
scenarios.when: a Secret exists with label `forge.c5c3.io/rotation-target` but no owner reference
scenarios.then: the mapper does NOT enqueue any Keystone CR
and_then: orphans can only appear from manual editing; behavior is safe-by-default

scenarios.name: Unrelated Secrets not enqueued more than before
scenarios.when: an unrelated Secret in the namespace changes
scenarios.then: the mapper behavior is unchanged from before CC-0081

requirements.description: The operator SHALL expose RBAC verbs `get`, `list`, `watch`, `patch`, `update`, `delete` on `core/secrets` in its existing ClusterRole/Role (no changes needed).
requirements.rationale: CC-0081: The apply + delete actions against the production and staging Secrets use the operator's existing privileged ServiceAccount. The Helm chart and `+kubebuilder:rbac` markers must continue to reflect the needed verbs.

scenarios.name: Existing operator RBAC is sufficient
scenarios.when: the operator reconcile performs PATCH on production Secret and DELETE on staging Secret
scenarios.then: no 403 occurs because the operator Role already includes `patch`, `update`, `delete` on secrets
and_then: no new `+kubebuilder:rbac` markers are required for this feature

scenarios.name: Helm chart rules unchanged
scenarios.when: `make manifests` is run after the refactor
scenarios.then: `operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/templates/_helpers.tpl` Secret-related rules produce the same verb set as before CC-0081

requirements.description: The rotation workflow SHOULD complete end-to-end (CronJob start → production Secret updated) in under 60 seconds under nominal conditions.
requirements.rationale: CC-0081: Credential rotation re-encrypts DB records with the new primary. The window between `credential_migrate` and the operator's apply should be minimized to avoid decryption failures in API pods.

scenarios.name: Watch-driven apply is prompt
scenarios.when: the CronJob PATCHes the staging Secret
scenarios.then: the operator reconcile observes the event and applies to production within <10 seconds in a healthy cluster
and_then: the 60s budget accommodates kubelet projection + controller queue latency

scenarios.name: Backpressure does not block the apply path
scenarios.when: the operator is reconciling multiple Keystones
scenarios.then: the per-Keystone reconcile queue still processes the staging Secret update

requirements.description: The operator SHALL emit Kubernetes events for every rotation-related state transition (Rotated, Rejected, AnnotationInvalid).
requirements.rationale: CC-0081: Observability is required for operators to audit and debug the split-write path.

scenarios.name: Successful apply event
scenarios.when: the operator successfully applies staged keys to the production Secret
scenarios.then: an event `Normal FernetKeysRotated keys=N` (or `CredentialKeysRotated`) is emitted on the Keystone CR

scenarios.name: Validation failure event
scenarios.when: validation rejects the staged keys
scenarios.then: an event `Warning RotationRejected reason=<validation error>` is emitted

scenarios.name: Events do not flood
scenarios.when: reconcile runs repeatedly with the same invalid staging Secret
scenarios.then: the event is emitted at most once per reconcile that observes the specific error state
and_then: the existing event-aggregation behavior of the recorder is relied on, not custom throttling

tasks.title: Define rotation staging constants + owner labels in new file `operators/keystone/internal/controller/rotation_staging.go`: constants `StagingSecretLabelKey="forge.c5c3.io/rotation-target"`, `RotationCompletedAnnotation="forge.c5c3.io/rotation-completed-at"`, helpers `fernetStagingSecretName(ks)` / `credentialStagingSecretName(ks)` that return `{ks.Name}-fernet-keys-rotation` / `{ks.Name}-credential-keys-rotation`. Unit test `rotation_staging_test.go` asserts the returned names follow the documented pattern for a range of Keystone names (REQ-003, REQ-004, REQ-005) (CC-0081).

Create `validateRotationOutput(keys map[string][]byte, minKeys, maxKeys int) error` in `operators/keystone/internal/controller/rotation_validation.go`: enforces (a) each value is 44-char base64url decoding to 32 bytes, (b) no duplicates (byte compare), (c) len(keys) in [minKeys, maxKeys]. Returns typed wrapped errors (ErrInvalidKeyFormat, ErrDuplicateKeys, ErrKeyCountOutOfRange). Add table-driven test covering each failure mode and an accept path using `generateFernetKey()` (REQ-006) (CC-0081).

Extend `ensureFernetRotationRBAC` in `reconcile_fernet.go`: replace the single PolicyRule {secrets, get+patch, [mainSecret]} with TWO PolicyRules — {secrets, [get], [mainSecret]} and {secrets, [get,patch], [stagingSecret]}. Add unit tests `TestEnsureFernetRotationRBAC_MainSecretIsReadOnly` and `TestEnsureFernetRotationRBAC_StagingSecretHasGetPatchOnly` using fake client. Also assert the Role is idempotent across reconciles (REQ-001, REQ-003) (CC-0081).

Mirror task 1.3 in `ensureCredentialRotationRBAC` in `reconcile_credential.go`: split the existing PolicyRule into two, one get-only on main, one get+patch on staging. Add symmetric unit tests in `reconcile_credential_test.go` (REQ-002, REQ-003) (CC-0081).

Add `ensureFernetStagingSecret(ctx, keystone) error` in `reconcile_fernet.go` — uses `controllerutil.CreateOrUpdate` to ensure a Secret named per task 1.1 exists with empty `Data`, label `rotation-target=fernet-keys`, common labels, and owner reference to the Keystone CR. Call it from `reconcileFernetKeys` after step 1 (Secret exists) and before step 2 (RBAC). Unit test `TestReconcileFernetKeys_CreatesEmptyStagingSecret` (REQ-004) (CC-0081).

Mirror task 2.1 for credential: `ensureCredentialStagingSecret` in `reconcile_credential.go`, called in `reconcileCredentialKeys`. Unit test `TestReconcileCredentialKeys_CreatesEmptyStagingSecret` (REQ-004) (CC-0081).

Implement `applyRotationOutput(ctx, keystone, stagingKey, mainKey, minKeys, maxKeys) (bool, error)` in `rotation_staging.go`: GET staging → if annotation absent, return (false, nil); parse annotation as RFC3339 (emit `RotationAnnotationInvalid` warning event on parse error, return (false, nil)); validate via `validateRotationOutput` (emit `RotationRejected` on failure, return (false, nil)); PATCH main Secret with strategic-merge-patch of `{"data": <staged data>}`; DELETE staging Secret. Emit Normal event `<Keys>Rotated` on success. Returns `applied=true` so the caller can short-circuit requeue. Unit tests cover each branch with a fake client (REQ-005, REQ-006, REQ-012) (CC-0081).

Wire `applyRotationOutput` into `reconcileFernetKeys`: immediately after ensureFernetStagingSecret, call applyRotationOutput for fernet (passing `minKeys=3`, `maxKeys=normalizedFernetMaxActiveKeys(ks)+1`). On success, emit `FernetKeysRotated` via r.Recorder and set FernetKeysReady True/FernetKeysAvailable. Existing unit tests updated; new test `TestReconcileFernetKeys_AppliesStagedKeysWhenAnnotationPresent` asserts end-to-end apply + staging deletion (REQ-005, REQ-006) (CC-0081).
Mirror task 2.4 in `reconcileCredentialKeys` using `normalizedCredentialMaxActiveKeys`. New test `TestReconcileCredentialKeys_AppliesStagedKeysWhenAnnotationPresent` (REQ-005, REQ-006) (CC-0081).

Extend `secretToKeystoneMapper` in `keystone_controller.go`: ensure Secrets with label `forge.c5c3.io/rotation-target` and an owner reference to a Keystone in the namespace enqueue that Keystone (already covered by `isOwnedBy` — add explicit test `TestSecretToKeystoneMapper_EnqueuesOnStagingSecretUpdate` and orphan test). No behavior change if owner references survive correctly; this is a verification task (REQ-009) (CC-0081).

Rewrite `operators/keystone/internal/controller/scripts/fernet_rotate.sh` so the Python heredoc (a) builds `data` from `/etc/keystone/fernet-keys/*` (unchanged), (b) uses new env var `SECRET_NAME` pointing at the staging Secret (same name, different value), (c) adds `metadata.annotations["forge.c5c3.io/rotation-completed-at"]` with `datetime.datetime.now(datetime.timezone.utc).isoformat(timespec="seconds").replace("+00:00","Z")` to the PATCH JSON body. Preserve SPDX header, `set -eu`, and the CC-0073 sync note. Update `TestFernetRotateScript_EmbeddedContent` to assert the annotation key appears and adjust the in-sync note (REQ-007) (CC-0081).

Mirror task 3.1 in `credential_rotate.sh`: same PATCH payload shape with annotation; preserve the `credential_rotate` → `credential_migrate` → PATCH order. Update `TestCredentialRotateScript_MigrateBeforePatch` to assert byte-offset ordering between the three keystone-manage/python3 invocations (REQ-008) (CC-0081).

In `fernetRotationCronJob` and `credentialRotationCronJob`, change the container `SECRET_NAME` env var value to the staging Secret name (via the helpers from task 1.1). Update existing `TestFernetRotationCronJob_PodSpec` / credential counterpart assertions for SECRET_NAME (REQ-007, REQ-008) (CC-0081).

Add envtest integration test `TestRotationApplyEndToEnd_EnvTest` in `integration_test.go`: create a Keystone CR → reconcile → assert staging Secret exists empty → patch staging Secret with valid keys + completion annotation → next reconcile → assert production Secret data equals staging data → assert staging Secret is deleted → assert `FernetKeysRotated` event (REQ-005, REQ-012) (CC-0081).

Add envtest integration test `TestRotationApplyRejectsMalformedKeys_EnvTest`: stage keys of length 32 (wrong) with valid annotation → reconcile → assert production Secret unchanged, staging Secret retained, `RotationRejected` event on the CR (REQ-006, REQ-012) (CC-0081).

Update `tests/e2e/keystone/fernet-rotation/chainsaw-test.yaml` with an additional step between manual rotation trigger and token-validation step: assert that `kubectl get role {name}-fernet-rotate` has NO `patch` verb in any rule containing the production Secret name, and that a `patch` verb exists in a rule containing `{name}-fernet-keys-rotation`. Preserve all existing steps (REQ-001, REQ-011) (CC-0081).

Update `tests/e2e/keystone/credential-rotation/chainsaw-test.yaml` symmetrically to task 4.3 for the credential rotation Role, asserting read-only on the production credential Secret and staging-Secret-scoped patch (REQ-002, REQ-011) (CC-0081).

Update `docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md`: add a subsection `Key Rotation RBAC Split (CC-0081)` under both the Fernet and credential reconciler sections describing (a) the staging Secret naming, (b) the completion annotation contract, (c) the operator's validation rules, (d) the RBAC verb matrix for CronJob SA vs operator SA. Add the new constants to the labels/annotations tables at the top of the file (REQ-001–REQ-010) (CC-0081).

Add/extend a How-to guide (prefer extending an existing keystone-rotation file if present; otherwise create `docs/how-to/keystone-key-rotation.md`) covering: trigger manual rotation, verify staging Secret completion annotation transiently appears, verify operator apply (event on CR), confirm production Secret data changed, recover from `RotationRejected` (inspect staging Secret + events). Include the new Feature ID CC-0081 in the frontmatter (REQ-012) (CC-0081).

review_criteria: BLOCKER: The Role created by `ensureFernetRotationRBAC` and `ensureCredentialRotationRBAC` grants NO write verbs (`patch`, `update`, `create`, `delete`, `deletecollection`, `*`) on the production keys Secrets — verified by the dedicated unit tests from tasks 1.3 and 1.4 (REQ-001, REQ-002).

BLOCKER: The operator does NOT apply staged keys to the production Secret unless the staging Secret carries a valid RFC3339 `forge.c5c3.io/rotation-completed-at` annotation AND `validateRotationOutput` passes — verified by the envtest from task 4.1 and 4.2 (REQ-005, REQ-006).
BLOCKER: `credential_rotate.sh` continues to run `credential_rotate` then `credential_migrate` then the PATCH, in that order — verified by the byte-offset assertion in the script test (REQ-008).
CRITICAL: Staging Secrets carry an owner reference to the Keystone CR so cluster-wide GC removes them on Keystone deletion (REQ-004).
CRITICAL: The CronJob `SECRET_NAME` env var points at the staging Secret name for both Fernet and credential rotators — verified by unit tests from task 3.3 (REQ-007, REQ-008).

CRITICAL: The end-user-observable behavior of `kubectl create job --from=cronjob/...` is unchanged — the existing e2e fernet-rotation and credential-rotation chainsaw tests pass after task 4.3 and 4.4 updates (REQ-011).
MINOR: A `FernetKeysRotated` / `CredentialKeysRotated` Normal event is emitted on successful apply, and a `RotationRejected` Warning event is emitted on validation failure (REQ-012).

MINOR: Documentation reflects the new architecture — `docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md` describes the staging Secret, validation rules, and RBAC matrix; a how-to covers manual rotation and RotationRejected recovery (tasks 5.1, 5.2).
MINOR: Every test, doc, and commit message references `CC-0081`; no dangling references to the old `get+patch` Role shape remain.
implementation_notes:
Architecture (Feature ID: CC-0081) — split CronJob compute from operator-owned write-to-production.

ASCII Architecture Diagram:

  +------------------+            +-----------------------+
  | CronJob Pod (SA: |  PATCH     | Staging Secret        |
  | fernet-rotate)   +----------->|  {name}-fernet-keys   |
  | RBAC:            | (data +    |    -rotation          |
  |   get main       |  annotation|  label: rotation-     |
  |   get/patch stg  |  ...)      |  target=fernet-keys   |
  +--------+---------+            |  owner: Keystone      |
           ^                      +-----------+-----------+
           |                                  |
           | read-only Secret volume          | watch (secretToKeystoneMapper)
           |                                  v
  +--------+------------------+    +-----------------------+
  | Production Secret         |<---+ Operator Reconcile    |
  |  {name}-fernet-keys       |    | (SA: keystone-op)     |
  | (PATCHed ONLY by operator)|    |  1. applyRotationOut  |
  +---------------------------+    |  2. validate keys     |
                                   |  3. PATCH main        |
                                   |  4. DELETE staging    |
                                   +-----------------------+

Data Flow:
  1. Reconcile: ensureStagingSecret (create empty, owner-ref)
  2. Reconcile: applyRotationOutput — if staging has completion annotation, validate+PATCH main+DELETE staging
  3. Reconcile: ensureRBAC (Role: get on main, get/patch on staging)
  4. Reconcile: ensureCronJob (SECRET_NAME=staging)
  5. CronJob: rotate in emptyDir → (credential only) migrate DB → PATCH staging Secret with data+annotation
  6. Watch event from staging Secret PATCH → Reconcile → applyRotationOutput (step 2) → production Secret updated
  7. Kubelet projects updated Secret into API pods → new keys active

Key design decisions:
  - STAGING SECRET (not ConfigMap): keys are sensitive material; Secret-typed resources get k8s RBAC isolation that ConfigMaps do not.
  - EMPTY-CREATED BY OPERATOR: the CronJob only needs `patch`, never `create` or `delete` — narrower verb surface.
  - ANNOTATION AS COMMIT MARKER: prevents the operator from observing a half-written staging Secret.
  - OPERATOR-SIDE VALIDATION: defense in depth — even a compromised CronJob cannot push malformed keys.
  - VALIDATION RULE 'count <= MaxActiveKeys+1': tolerates the transient state keystone-manage produces between adding the new primary and pruning the oldest key.
  - OPERATOR ALREADY HAS patch/delete ON SECRETS: no new +kubebuilder:rbac markers needed (REQ-010).
  - WATCH PATH UNCHANGED: secretToKeystoneMapper already enqueues Keystones for owned Secrets via isOwnedBy; staging Secret just rides that path (task 2.6 is verification, not new wiring).

Pitfalls to avoid:
  - Do NOT forget the completion-annotation gate. Without it, reconcile can race the CronJob's writes and see a half-populated staging Secret.
  - Do NOT relax validation to 'len(keys) == MaxActiveKeys' exactly — keystone-manage transiently produces N+1 between add and prune.
  - Do NOT add `list` or `watch` on Secrets to the CronJob Role — the CronJob reads via projected volume.
  - RoleRef on RoleBinding is IMMUTABLE — existing code already guards with `if rb.RoleRef.Name == ''`, preserve that check.
  - For CREDENTIAL rotation, credential_migrate MUST run before the staging PATCH — the DB is re-encrypted with the new primary key and the production Secret update must follow shortly after to minimize the decryption-failure window (REQ-008, REQ-011).
  - CronJob lacks `delete` on staging Secret — if a past rotation's staging Secret is left over, the OPERATOR (not the CronJob) must delete it on successful apply (task 2.3 handles this).

Existing patterns reused:
  - Sub-reconciler condition contract with ObservedGeneration (sub-reconciler-condition-contract-with-observedgeneration.md)
  - Kubernetes event emission alongside condition setting (kubernetes-event-emission-alongside-condition-setting-with-fakerecorder-test-ass.md)
  - Manual Get/Create/Update with SetControllerReference (manual-getcreateupdate-with-setcontrollerreference.md)
  - Shared restrictedSecurityContext helper for PSS (unchanged — CronJob container still uses it)
  - Chainsaw E2E test suite structure (chainsaw-e2e-test-suite-structure-with-jmespath-condition-assertions.md)
  - Immutability verification test for map-returning pure functions — applies to rotation_staging helpers
